content: {"question": "What do published effectiveness, adherence, and retention data from commercial intermittent-fasting mobile applications (Zero, Fastic, LIFE Fasting Tracker, Simple, DoFasting) actually show for real-world user outcomes over 12-24 weeks, and how do these real-world effect sizes compare to the weight-loss magnitudes reported in peer-reviewed RCTs that the apps cite in their marketing?", "search_queries": ["Zero fasting app real-world weight loss outcomes retention study", "intermittent fasting mobile application adherence dropout rate randomized trial", "digital health coaching time-restricted eating effectiveness commercial product", "fasting app 12 week weight change self-report observational", "consumer intermittent fasting product claims peer reviewed comparison"], "perspective": "Industry"}
